summary: Matrix recording captures a hot 1979 Pittsburgh show with standout Sugaree and an all‑time favorite Althea.
review: The matrix source provides a decent, if not pristine, listening experience—enough to hear Jerry’s rescuing guitar work on Sugaree despite a few sour notes in Jack Straw. Fans consistently point to the take‑off on Sugaree and the long, mellow Althea as highlights of an otherwise solid performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the show (Sugaree take‑off, Althea) and give 4–5 stars, while noting recording is only fair compared to other 1979 tapes; weighted average yields a solid four‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix blend with acceptable clarity; a few sour notes on Jack Straw and overall fidelity not as high as other 1979 recordings, but sound is still listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert reportedly takes off on Sugaree and maintains momentum, with Althea serving as a long, mellow centerpiece; some feel the performance isn’t consistently 'on' throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Sour notes noted on Jack Straw but his guitar work on Sugaree is praised for rescuing the moment.
